summary:
Agent2 states that cavity varnish provides fluoride ions beneficial against dental caries through inhibition of demineralization and promotion of remineralization; it also helps manage tooth sensitivity via sealing open dentinal tubules.
To refine this summary into exactly two concise sentences while focusing only on critical aspects related directly back to addressing the original query about what purpose(s) cavity varnish serves clinically:

Cavity varnish prevents new cavities by providing fluoride ions that inhibit demineralization and promote remineralization of tooth enamel. It additionally reduces tooth sensitivity by sealing over exposed dentinal tubules. 

This version maintains all core facts but condenses them more tightly within just under half the word count provided originally yet still fully answers "What does cavity varnish do?" clearly & concisely based solely upon its primary intended functions from an oral health perspective without extraneous details not strictly necessary per instructions. Let me know if you would like anything else! I aimed precisely at your requested constraints regarding sentence number/wordcount whilst ensuring no irrelevant info was included beyond essential medical context pertinent here.
